description: You are a math teacher creating a 'Real World Calculus' lesson on optimizing a rugby conversion kick. Using the goal width provided in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/data/rugby_dimensions.txt and a try location 10 meters to the side of the nearest goal post, model the scenario in GeoGebra. Create a function graph showing how the angle subtended by the goal posts changes as a kicker moves back perpendicularly from the try location. Identify and mark the optimal kicking distance where this angle is maximized. Save the project as 'rugby_optimization.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/.
